article_meta.title: 4 Republicans defy Speaker Johnson to force House vote on extending ACA subsidies The Associated PressAP17 Dec 2025 Four

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for an article that contains several instances of author-level bias in the prose. While this is largely an AP wire story with relatively neutral reporting, there are some clear instances of loaded language and framing in the author's own prose that should have been flagged.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero attribution rule violations. No quoted text was flagged as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged at all, so there are zero false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several defensible bias instances in the author's own prose: (1) 'The stunning move' - Sensationalism/Word Choice, author's own characterization; (2) 'soaring monthly premiums that millions of people will soon endure' - Sensationalism/Emotionalism and Word Choice, 'soaring' and 'endure' are loaded terms in author prose; (3) 'pushing it to the magic number of 218' - Word Choice, 'magic number' is informal/loaded; (4) 'The revolt against GOP leadership' - Word Choice, 'revolt' is dramatic framing by the author; (5) 'an arduous climb' - Word Choice/Subjective Qualifying Adjective, loaded characterization of legislative prospects; (6) 'an encouraging sign for Democrats' - Slant, author frames events positively from Democrats' perspective. While some could be debated, 'stunning move,' 'soaring...endure,' and 'encouraging sign for Democrats' are fairly clear instances of author bias.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No bias types were applied since the array was empty, so no incorrect labels were used.
